quotes.text: I've read every page of this bill. Well — every page that wasn't a cross-reference to another bill, which, if you followed it all the way down, basically just told you to read the first bill again.
quotes.speaker: Bernie Sanders
quotes.context: Town hall at the University of Vermont, Burlington, October 2019
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Bernie's exasperated-professor tone and his genuine obsession with legislative fine print make this land perfectly. The subtle giveaway is that Bernie almost never makes self-deprecating process jokes; he usually just barrels into the substance.

quotes.text: My mother called me after the debate. She said I did well. Then she said my tie was wrong. Then she talked about the tie for eleven minutes. My poll numbers she mentioned once.
quotes.speaker: Marco Rubio
quotes.context: Interview on The Hugh Hewitt Show, nationally syndicated, October 2015
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Rubio regularly invokes his Cuban-American family in grounded, warm anecdotes, and the specific maternal-criticism-over-substance angle fits his self-deprecating style. The very specific 'eleven minutes' is the tell — real anecdotes almost never come with that kind of precision.

quotes.text: The Internet is not something that you just dump something on. It's not a big truck. It's a series of tubes.
quotes.speaker: Ted Stevens
quotes.context: Republican U.S. Senator from Alaska, then chairman of the Senate Commerce Committee, attempting to describe how the internet works during a June 28, 2006 committee debate on net neutrality.

quotes.text: I've been called a lot of things over the years. Some of them required me to look up words I didn't know. A few of them I thought were actually pretty good.
quotes.speaker: Dick Cheney
quotes.context: Address at the Gerald R. Ford School of Public Policy, University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, September 2011
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Cheney's humor, when it exists, is bone-dry and self-aware about his polarizing image, and he genuinely has used that self-awareness in post-vice-presidency speeches. The giveaway is the final clause; Cheney would almost certainly not admit, even jokingly, that an insult landed well.

quotes.text: Tonight we come together for one of the most joyous and glorious milestones of all time,
quotes.speaker: Donald Trump
quotes.context: Trump's late-night speech marking America's 250th anniversary, July 4th celebrations

source.title: Politics — Trump celebrates America’s 250th anniversary in weather-delayed, sometimes parti

quotes.text: I will not make age an issue of this campaign. I am not going to exploit, for political purposes, my opponent's youth and inexperience.
quotes.speaker: Ronald Reagan
quotes.context: His famous comeback at the 1984 debate against Walter Mondale

source.title: CPD: October 21, 1984 Debate Transcript | Commission on Presidential Debates
